name: Training Readiness & Recovery
description: Turn how you feel today plus any recovery signals into one conservative, provider-neutral Universal AI Prompt that helps you decide whether to train as planned, modify the session, do light recovery only, rest and reassess, seek non-emergency medical advice, or stop and seek urgent care. Decision support only — it does not diagnose, and it never claims a wearable score makes training safe.
inputHint: Describe how you feel and what you were planning to train (e.g. "Fighting a cold, was going to lift heavy legs — train or rest?"). Add symptoms, sleep, soreness, or HRV/resting-HR vs your baseline if you have them. Severe symptoms need professional or emergency care, not this prompt.
assumptions: Your request is required; every symptom, recovery, and wearable detail is optional and used only when you provide it
The output is one provider-neutral Universal AI Prompt — no AI is called here and nothing leaves this device
This is decision support, not a diagnosis, and not a medical device; it does not prescribe medication or treatment
Wearable readiness scores and HRV are supporting signals only — a normal score never overrides real symptoms like chest pain or trouble breathing

Health Profile context is opt-in; with it excluded, no profile facts appear. When included, only a readiness-relevant field whitelist is used — nutrition macros, body metrics, medications, supplements, and free-text notes are excluded
The workflow never selects a diagnosis and never promises certainty; severe or red-flag symptoms are routed to professional or emergency care
nextAction: Copy the Universal AI Prompt, paste it into ChatGPT or Claude, and add any extra detail there. For severe or emergency symptoms, seek professional or emergency care instead.
